title: koa-session with secure: false — the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP and can be sniffed

params.passDetail: koa-session sets secure: true — the session cookie is only ever transmitted over HTTPS.
params.failDetail: koa-session is configured with secure: false, so the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP. On any non-TLS hop an attacker can read the session and hijack the authenticated session. Set secure: true in production (with app.proxy = true when TLS terminates at a proxy).
params.absentCallDetail: No koa-session session() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: koa-session does not set secure; verify production forces HTTPS-only cookies (secure: true).

provenance.note: secure: false is copied from local-dev config (no HTTPS) into production, sending the koa session cookie in the clear where it can be sniffed and replayed.
